mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the process of running Kubernetes on AWS. It provides a highly available and scalable environment for deploying containerized applications while managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install AWS CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the AWS CLI, which is necessary for managing AWS resources.

```bash

curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o "awscliv2.zip"

unzip awscliv2.zip

sudo ./aws/install

aws --version

```

> Download the AWS CLI installer.
> Unzip the downloaded installer.
> Install the AWS CLI.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your AWS account ID.
commands: aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the newly created EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

```

> Update kubeconfig for the EKS cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the EKS cluster are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the cluster nodes.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler-example.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/main/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.yaml

```

> Deploy the Cluster Autoscaler.
> Deploy the CloudWatch agent for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

```

> Delete the EKS cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources are cleaned up.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -n 1 aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer

```

> Delete any load balancers associated with the cluster.
commands: aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -n 1 aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer

upgrade.title: Upgrade EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster resources. This step upgrades the EKS cluster to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --region us-west-2

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> Upgrade the EKS cluster.
> Verify that the nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig is not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command: aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

troubleshooting.title: Error: No resources found in default namespace
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that no resources have been deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy an application to the cluster using kubectl apply -f <your-app.yaml>

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not running or the kubeconfig is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with aws eks describe-cluster --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient capacity in your availability zone
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the specified availability zone.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different availability zone or increase the instance type.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain APIs related to networking.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the Ready state.
codeSnippets: aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
